compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
rebar accretes back-compat aliases; entering BETA is the cheapest window to remove them before a larger user base depends on them. Maintainer chose a MIGRATE-FIRST clean break (pre-1.0, semver-permissible): migrate THIS repo to canonical names and confirm no internal use, THEN remove the shims. (DE7 — verified; the config.py:429-458 registry is the authoritative source.)

Citations: `src/rebar/config.py:429-458` (deprecation registry with v1.0.0 horizons).

## What
Two sequenced steps.

STEP 1 — migrate this repo + confirm no internal old-name use:
- Switch this repo's own config/env/library usage to canonical names.
- Grep the whole repo (src, tests, docs, CI, rebar.toml, scripts) to confirm nothing internal still sets/uses an old name.

STEP 2 — remove the 7 scheduled shims + handling code + update registry/docs:
- env `REBAR_PUSH` -> `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH`
- env `TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR` -> `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR`
- env `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` -> `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`
- cfg `verify.require_verdict_for_close` -> `verify.require_signature_for_close`
- cfg flat `.rebar/config.conf` reader -> `rebar.toml`
- lib `edit_ticket(tags=...)` -> `set_tags`/`add_tags`/`remove_tags`
- lib `rebar.list_epics()` -> `list_tickets(ticket_type='epic')`
- CLI `--verdict-hash` (ignored) — remove.
NOTE: leave the PERMANENT ergonomic renames (`REBAR_NO_SYNC`, `COMPACT_THRESHOLD`, `SCRATCH_BASE_DIR`, `REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT`, etc.) — they are not scheduled for removal. This list may EXPAND from the pre-beta deprecation sweep (see linked findings).

## Scope
Breaking change for external consumers still on old names (accepted, pre-1.0). Update release-notes with a BREAKING section. Coordinate STEP 2 after STEP 1 is verified clean.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] This repo uses only canonical names (config/env/lib/CLI); repo-wide grep confirms no internal old-name use
- [ ] The 7 scheduled shims + their handling code are removed
- [ ] The config.py deprecation registry + docs updated to reflect removals
- [ ] Permanent ergonomic renames are retained (not removed)
- [ ] release-notes records the breaking change
compiled_state.title: Remove deprecated aliases pre-beta (migrate this repo first, then drop shims)
